schema_notes: Per-billionaire record matching Ellison v3 reference. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events scoped by event_role. Tier classification happens downstream.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research + regen_v3@sha256-7001c9c7cdd64155 + regen_v3@sha256-7fc2c3a0f9dc1c59

person.wealth_source: Hedge fund founder — SAC Capital Advisors (1992-2016); Point72 Asset Management (2014-present, ~$45.7B AUM). Owner of NY Mets (acquired controlling stake Sept 2020 for $2.4B). Major art collector.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 list: $23B, ranked 111th globally (up $1.7B YoY per Hartford Business Journal). Several secondary sources still citing $21-21.3B carryover from 2024-2025. Consensus band $21-23B.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: Hedge-fund GP fortune has higher liquidity than operator-founder wealth (Ellison/Zuckerberg). Point72 is private but Cohen's stake is cashable via fund distributions. Offset: $2.4B locked in Mets (illiquid franchise asset, not a philanthropy vehicle). Art collection (reportedly $1B+) is semi-liquid. Apply 50% liquidity discount — between hedge-fund GP norm (60-70%) and public-stock-founder norm (20-30%).

cited_events.note: 2024 charitable disbursements $129.7M; assets $537.2M — payout ratio ~24%, well above 5% minimum.

2013 is the year of the $1.8B SAC Capital insider-trading plea; foundation giving continued uninterrupted. $248M revenue into foundation that year (largest single-year funding) — Cohen pre-funded the foundation's balance sheet from $0.6M assets (2011) to $280M (2013) even as criminal case unfolded.

cited_events.donor_entity: Cohen Veterans Network Inc (public charity, separate 501(c)(3))
cited_events.dedup_note: CVN is a separately-incorporated public charity operating the 22-clinic mental-health network. Its revenue comes primarily from SACF grants and cost-sharing with treated veterans. Counting CVN expenses as additive to SACF would DOUBLE-COUNT: SACF grants to CVN are already inside SACF's $129.7M 2024 disbursements. Therefore CVN expenses are NOT added to observable_giving — listed here for transparency. The operational spend is the evidence that SACF dollars actually reach veterans.

cited_events.donor_entity: Cohen Veterans Bioscience Inc (public charity, separate 501(c)(3))
cited_events.dedup_note: CVB is a separately-incorporated 501(c)(3) research org focused on PTSD/TBI biomarkers. Similar to CVN: its program expenses are downstream of SACF grants — NOT additive. Listed for transparency on operational footprint. CEO Magali Haas compensation $497K (2024) is a notable figure for a small research shop — not HIGH_COMP relative to program budget (~3.5%) but worth flagging.

cited_events.recipient: LaGuardia Community College (CUNY) — Cohen Career Collective
cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL: This $116.2M grant was awarded by SACF and is therefore almost certainly INSIDE the SACF 2024 $129.7M disbursement total — not additive. Marked direct_gift for narrative clarity but MUST NOT be double-counted against cohen_sacf_2024. Largest-ever gift to a US community college per multiple sources.

cited_events.recipient: Museum of Modern Art — expansion capital campaign
cited_events.dedup_note: Gift given THROUGH SACF per Art News and Bloomberg — therefore rolled into SACF grants_out. Not additive. Cohen joined MoMA trustee board in 2016; gift surfaced a year later. Named Steven and Alexandra Cohen Center for Special Exhibitions (sixth-floor gallery).

cited_events.donor_entity: Steven & Alexandra Cohen Foundation (likely — Wikipedia cites 'Cohen')
cited_events.recipient: Morgan Stanley Children's Hospital of NewYork-Presbyterian
cited_events.dedup_note: Pre-dates SACF's major-grant era (SACF assets were <$1M through 2011). Could have been a personal gift OR from an earlier foundation vehicle. Insufficient source specificity to confirm. Left here for narrative continuity; not counted toward observable (would fall in years with incomplete SACF data). Hospital was renamed 'Morgan Stanley Children's Hospital' ~2003; Cohen gift named the Steven and Alexandra Cohen Pediatric Emergency Department.

cited_events.status_note: 2016 announcement pledged $275M to stand up a national network of free mental-health clinics for veterans. CVN revenue 2015-2024 aggregate (from 990s): ~$342M — the pledge has been funded and then some. Counting mechanics: the flows into CVN show up as grants_out from SACF (primary funder), so already captured in SACF rollup. NOT double-counted here.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: 'More than $100M' cumulative per reporting — exact figure not disclosed. Single largest disclosed year: $13.35M in 2011 (LittleSis).
cited_events.dedup_note: Cohen served on Robin Hood board 2004-2014 (stepped down to emeritus). Gifts likely flowed both personally and through SACF. Portion overlapping with SACF 2011-2014 grant-outs is already captured. Treat this row as narrative-only; do not add.

cited_events.recipient: Various (990-PF aggregate; recipient detail not parsed)
cited_events.note: ProPublica 990-PF aggregate for fiscal-year 2015; end-year assets $459.3M; payout rate 19.1%.

cited_events.recipient: Various (990-PF aggregate; recipient detail not parsed)
cited_events.note: ProPublica 990-PF aggregate for fiscal-year 2023; end-year assets $527.6M; payout rate 24.9%.

pledges_and_announcements.verification_method: Searched givingpledge.org/pledgers page; Steve Cohen not listed. Direct URL /pledger/steve-cohen returns 404.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Cohen has NOT signed the Giving Pledge. Red flag NO_GIVING_PLEDGE_SIGNATURE applies — but note this flag is weaker than PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (e.g., Ellison), because Cohen has not made the 50%+ commitment at all. Interpret in context: his lifetime giving posture ($1.5B+ SACF cumulative per foundation website) is higher than most Giving Pledge signatories' realized giving, so the absence of a pledge is more accurately 'did not overcommit' than 'underperforming the commitment.'

pledges_and_announcements.note: Foundation website claims 'over $1.5B' in cumulative grantmaking since 2001. Reconciliation against 990-PF data: sum of available disbursements 2011-2024 = ~$1.021B. Adding pre-2011 giving (foundation existed from 2001 but at smaller scale) and 2015-2018 gap years (990 data shown in fetch covers 2011-2015 + 2019-2024 as visible rows) plausibly bridges to $1.5B claim. Credible self-report; not counted additive.

pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: NOT philanthropy. Included for context on liquid-wealth deployment. Per standard Forbes/IPS methodology, for-profit sports franchise purchase does not count as charitable giving. Also notable: this $2.4B is ~10% of current net worth tied up in an illiquid asset — relevant to liquidity calculation.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616: political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Minimum floor from press aggregation — NOT a comprehensive FEC bulk pull. Confirmed items: $57K Dem Senatorial Cmte 2008; $28K NRSC 2008; $1.5M Rep Governors Assoc 2010; $30.4K NRSC 2012; $2.85M America Leads super-PAC (Christie 2016); $2M Senate Leadership Fund 2017; $1M Congressional Leadership Fund 2017; $1M Trump inaugural 2017. Fox Business (2025) reports 'ramping up GOP donations' in 2024-2025 cycle but specific figures not retrieved. Dollar figure will tighten when FEC API direct-pull runs.
political_giving.pattern_note: Cohen gave bipartisan through 2008; shifted to near-exclusively Republican after 2010 SEC investigation of SAC Capital began. Hasn't given to 'virtually a single Democrat on the federal level since 2010' (Fox Business 2025). Pattern strongly consistent with defensive regulatory posture, not ideology.

sources.used_for: 2024-2025 GOP donation surge, historical donation timeline
Senate Leadership Fund $2M, Congressional Leadership Fund $1M (2017)
Donor-detail page (Steven A. Cohen, ID U0000003829)

foundations_active.name: Steven & Alexandra Cohen Foundation Inc
foundations_active.officers_compensation: ZERO_COMP (Steven A Cohen VP/Treasurer, Alexandra M Cohen President/Secretary, Joshua Cohen Director — all $0)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 charitable disbursements $129.7M / assets $537.2M = 24%. Far above 5% legal minimum. No LOW_PAYOUT flag. Multi-year pattern shows foundation functions as a flow-through, not an endowment build-up: assets have been stable at ~$520M since 2019 while disbursements grew from $102.6M to $129.7M.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT_YET_VERIFIED — would require parsing grant recipients list. Foundation website names specific grantees (LaGuardia, CVN, CVB, MoMA, many smaller) suggesting direct grantmaking rather than DAF warehousing, but unverified from ProPublica summary page.

operating_charities_funded.name: Cohen Veterans Network Inc (CVN)
operating_charities_funded.focus: 22-clinic national network delivering free mental-health care to post-9/11 veterans and families
operating_charities_funded.scale: 22 clinics across 21 states; 82,000+ clients served April 2016-March 2025
operating_charities_funded.controversy_note: ProPublica 2018 investigation documented operational friction: Los Angeles clinic closed <1 year after opening; staff allegedly told to prioritize healthier patients over homeless veterans. VA integration contested. Does not undermine the scale of the infrastructure built ($342M+ aggregate operational spend 2015-2024), but tempers 'clean hero' framing.

operating_charities_funded.name: Cohen Veterans Bioscience Inc (CVB)
operating_charities_funded.focus: Biomarker research for PTSD, TBI, and trauma-related brain disorders
operating_charities_funded.note: CVB revenue has declined sharply from $21M (2019) peak to $3.4M (2025 partial). Operating at expense > revenue since at least 2019. Whether Cohen is winding down CVB or sustaining a research program through net-asset drawdown is ambiguous. Flag for follow-up.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Absence-of-evidence only. Cohen's giving pattern via named public grants to named recipients (LaGuardia, MoMA, CVN, CVB) suggests low reliance on DAFs, but this is inference, not verification.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Point72 Asset Management is Cohen's hedge-fund business, NOT a philanthropic LLC. Not analogous to CZI / Ballmer Group / Emerson Collective.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. No public reporting flags offshore structure for Cohen.

red_flags.evidence: Cohen is not listed at givingpledge.org/pledgers; /pledger/steve-cohen returns 404. At age 69 and $23B net worth, non-participation is notable but the flag is softer than PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED since he has not made and broken a 50% commitment.

Cohen's giving was bipartisan through 2008 and flipped to near-exclusively Republican after 2010 SEC investigation. Fox Business (2025) reports renewed GOP donation surge coinciding with hedge-fund comeback. Pattern suggests regulatory-defensive, not ideological. NBER W26616 substitution: ~$0.33 charitable giving forgone per $1 political.

SAC Capital pleaded guilty and paid $1.8B in criminal fines (2013) — largest insider-trading fine in US history. Cohen personally settled SEC civil case (2016) with 2-year supervisory ban. Not a philanthropy red flag per se, but relevant to reputational-laundering hypothesis: foundation assets grew from $0.6M (2011) to $280M (2013) during criminal case, and CVN/CVB were launched (2015) immediately after Point72 relaunch. Reader should weigh whether the mental-health infrastructure is genuine impact, reputation-rebuild, or both (most likely: both).

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (SACF 2024 payout 24% of assets, well above 5% minimum)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP at foundation (all SACF officers zero comp). CVN/CVB CEO comp is at operating-charity scale, not foundation scale.
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity identified; not confirmed absent)
PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (did not sign Giving Pledge — can't break what wasn't made)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(SACF 990-PF charitable disbursements 2011-2024 for years with data: 2011-2015 + 2019-2024, i.e. 42.5+43.2+43.5+51.0+60.1+102.6+92.0+109.7+122.5+124.4+129.7). 2016-2018 SACF data not retrieved (gap years). Excludes: CVN/CVB operational expenses (downstream of SACF — double-count risk); Mets acquisition; political giving; $50M Morgan Stanley Children's 2008 (unconfirmed vehicle).
observable_giving_todo: Pull SACF 990-PF for 2016, 2017, 2018 — plausibly add $150-250M to observable.
Parse SACF 2024 Part XV grant recipient list to verify LaGuardia $116M is inside the $129.7M (high confidence yes).
Verify the $50M 2008 Morgan Stanley Children's Hospital gift's vehicle — add to observable if confirmed direct.
Run FEC bulk pull against Steven A. Cohen's name/address to tighten political total.
Parse SACF grant recipient list for DAF intermediaries (confirm or refute DAF_TRANSFERS flag).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.50 × $23B = $11.5B; tenure cap saturated (25 years). Expected = $1.15B. Strict 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence; the visible grantmaking pattern (named public recipients) argues against significant DAF warehousing. Zero uplift.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked. Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously sometimes. Cohen has a documented preference for naming recipient-side (MoMA gallery named, CVN/CVB are name-attached, LaGuardia building named), which WEAKENS the anonymous-cohort hypothesis for him. Apply ~1% of observable giving as a conservative hidden upper: ~$115M over lifetime. Well-below cohort median.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly documented religious-tithing commitment. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: No disclosed charitable trust structure. Zero envelope.
hidden_upper_usd.gap_years_method: SACF 2016-2018 data not yet retrieved; interpolating between $60M (2015) and $102.6M (2019) at ~$70M/yr/yr gives ~$200-220M additional for those three years.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Cohen fails this test decisively (ratio ~0.98, approaching 1.0). He is an AT-OR-NEAR 10%-norm giver given liquid wealth. The case for Tier B rather than 'Not Low' is that (a) he has not signed the Giving Pledge (no forward commitment), (b) political giving pattern is defensive, (c) SAC history adds context. But observable giving itself is the strongest in this cohort of US-based hedge-fund billionaires at this wealth level.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Cohen is the canonical example of 'built genuine infrastructure — CVN mental-health clinics, LaGuardia workforce center, MoMA capital — without Giving Pledge signaling.' Observable giving ratio 3.5% of current net worth is an order of magnitude higher than Ellison's 0.2%. Readers should not confuse the absence of a pledge with absence of giving. Flag the SAC history and political-donation defensiveness for context, not as giving-denominator adjustments.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: v2-formula placeholder for pipeline continuity. giving_gap = 1 - 0.706 = 0.294 × 40 = 11.8. tenure saturated. no pledge signed so pledge_breach = 0 (the formula penalizes signed-but-unfulfilled, not non-signers). 2 of 3 red flags × 5 = 10. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only.
